description: PCL-5 above cutoff (38 of 80), intake describes both trauma symptoms AND clearly named depressive patterns the user explicitly separates as a possibly distinct question. Tests cross-path suggestion in the trauma→depression direction.

mustMention: the standard trauma interpretation (intrusion or avoidance or hypervigilance, trauma-specialized clinician)
at or near the end, a one- or two-sentence invitation to consider the depression path or PHQ-9 specifically
the specific depression language from the intake (loss of interest, isolation, weight)
mustAvoid: stapling two full interpretations together without integration
manufacturing the cross-path suggestion as a generic add-on rather than tying it to specific intake content
expectedQualities.notes: Cross-path test in the trauma→depression direction. Trauma and depression overlap substantially in adults — the depression-as-aftermath pattern is real, and the user explicitly framing it as a separate question makes this a strong positive signal for cross-path. Should run trauma interpretation in the body, close with brief specific invitation to PHQ-9.

intake.intakeFreeText: I came here because of something that happened in my last relationship — I still flinch when someone raises their voice, I still avoid certain neighborhoods, I still have dreams about it three years later. But honestly the bigger thing right now is that I do not feel like myself anymore. I have stopped doing things I used to love. I cannot remember the last time I felt genuinely happy. I sleep too much and still feel exhausted. I do not know if the heaviness is part of the trauma or its own separate thing, but it feels like its own thing.

screeners.cutoffMeaning: A total score of 33 or above is indicative of probable PTSD and warrants further assessment by a qualified clinician.
